body.title: SITUATE: affected_files_from_description accepts declared new-file paths (not just existing)
body.result: Shipped in 58d7b4da. Split _parse_file_list (strict, tier.2 guard) from _parse_declared_file_list (declared-author-trusted, returns all+new). _affected_files_from_description now accepts declared-new paths. pe_situate writes basket.new_files marker so IMPLEMENT knows create-vs-edit. 8 new tests. 188 pe_chain regression pass. This removes the biggest SITUATE-returns-0 source for new-file Igor tickets — direct Igor-completes-one unblocker.
body.description:
Discovered while auditing Igor's stuck tickets today. Every Igor ticket that proposes a NEW file (e.g. T-no-sqlite-enforcement: 'new lab/claudecode/check_no_sqlite.py', T-trace-to-miss-report: 'new lab/claudecode/engram_tools/trace_miss_report.py') has SITUATE return 0 files. Root: _parse_file_list (line 689) requires paths to exist under _REPO_ROOT to be accepted. _affected_files_from_description reuses _parse_file_list, so declared-new paths are silently filtered out. Consequence: every new-file ticket fails the affected_files_field fast path, falls through to tier.2 which then hallucinates brainstem/core_patterns.py.

Fix: split the strict existence-check path (for tier.2 returns where we want to reject hallucinated paths) from the Affected-files-field parse (where we trust the ticket author). Introduce _parse_declared_file_list that accepts any repo-relative path ending in .py (or containing /) regardless of filesystem existence. _affected_files_from_description uses that. _parse_file_list stays strict for the tier.2 guard path. Mark declared-new paths with a basket flag so IMPLEMENT can create them instead of expecting an edit target.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (line 689 _parse_file_list stays; add _parse_declared_file_list; update _affected_files_from_description).
**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — parser split + Affected-files-field accepts new files + basket marker for new paths. OUT — IMPLEMENT create-new-file logic (separate concern; tier.2 guard stays existence-strict).
**Test plan:** (a) _affected_files_from_description with description naming a non-existent .py path returns that path; (b) _parse_file_list still rejects non-existent paths from tier.2 output; (c) SITUATE integration with a ticket naming new file populates plan_files + sets a new-file marker in basket.
